runtime_evidence.typed_guard: LlamaModelConfig::from_gguf rejects Mixtral/MoE metadata before dense tensor binding
runtime_evidence.api_load: /api/models/load exposes unsupported_runtime.code=unsupported_model_architecture while tokenizer metadata remains available
runtime_evidence.api_generation: /api/generation/sessions returns unsupported_model_architecture with Mixtral MoE top-k expert-routing message
runtime_evidence.ubuntu_validation: Clean Ubuntu checkout at commit 5f270ab reproduced the typed Mixtral MoE unsupported API behavior using a 64 MiB sparse-header GGUF; targeted model_binding/tokenizer/capabilities tests passed, and real Mixtral tokenizer parity passed with MIXTRAL_GGUF pointing at the sparse header.
runtime_evidence.rss_note: debug server, sparse-header GGUF metadata/tokenizer load only; no full tensor payload or generation parity attempted

tokenizer_evidence.reference_tool: llama.cpp llama-tokenize --ids against exact-row sparse GGUF header

next_step: implement the smallest top-k expert routing path and prove one deterministic prompt only after MoE routing is unit-tested
